author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XII. 25, 26.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And Jesus knew their thoughts, and said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself shall be brought to desolation; and every city or house divided against itself, shall not stand: and if Satan cast out Satan, he is divided against himself; how shall then his kingdom stand?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even before now they had accused Him of this, that “by Beelzebub He casteth out the devils.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But whereas then He did not rebuke them, allowing them both to know His power by His more numerous miracles, and by His teaching to learn His majesty: now, since they continued saying the same, He proceeds also to rebuke them, showing His Godhead by this first, that He made their secrets public; and secondly, by the very act of casting out the devils with ease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And indeed the accusation too was very shameless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because, as I have said, envy seeks not what to say, but only that it may say somewhat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet for all that, not even so did Christ despise them, but defends Himself with the forbearance proper to Him, teaching us to be meek to our enemies; and though they say such things, as we are neither conscious of, nor have they any the least probability, not to be disturbed, nor troubled, but with all long suffering to render them an account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This then He did most especially on that very occasion, affording the strongest proof, that the things were false that were said by them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither was it a demoniac’s part to exhibit so much meekness; it was not a demoniac’s part to know men’s secrets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, in truth, both because of the exceeding impudence of such a suspicion, and because of the fear of the multitude, they durst not publicly make these charges, but were turning them in their mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He, to show them that He knew all that likewise, doth not set down the accusation, nor doth He expose their wickedness; but the refutation He adds, leaving it to the conscience of them that had said it to convict them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For on one thing only was He bent, to do good to them that were sinning, not to expose them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet surely, if He had been minded to extend his speech in length, and to make them ridiculous, and withal to have exacted of them also the most extreme penalty, there was nothing to hinder Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless He put aside all these things, and looked to one object only, not to render them more contentious, but more candid, and so to dispose them better toward amendment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then doth He plead with them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not by allegation out of the Scriptures (for they would not so much as attend, but were sure rather to distort their meaning), but by the events of ordinary life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “every kingdom,” saith He, “divided against itself shall not stand; and a city and a house, if it be divided, is soon dissolved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the wars from without are not so ruinous as the civil ones.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and this is the case in bodies too; it is the case even in all things; but for this time He takes His illustration from those that are more publicly known.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet, what is there more powerful on earth than a kingdom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nothing, but nevertheless it perishes if in dissension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if in that case one throw the blame on the great burden of the affairs thereof, as breaking down by its own weight; what wouldest thou say of a city? and what of a house?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, whether it be a small thing, or a great, if at dissension with itself, it perishes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If then I, having a devil, do by him cast out the devils, there is dissension and fighting among devils, and they take their stand one against another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if they stand one against another, their strength is wasted and destroyed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For if Satan cast out Satan” (and He said not “the devils,” implying their great unanimity one with another), “he is then divided against himself;” so He speaks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if he be divided, he is become weaker, and is ruined; and if he be ruined, how can he cast out another?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how great the absurdity of the accusation, how great the folly, the inconsistency?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since it is not for the same persons to say first, that He stands, and casts out devils, and then to say, that He stands by that, which it was likely would be the cause of His undoing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This then being the first refutation, the next after it is that which relates to the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not always in one way only, but also in a second and third, He solves their objections, being minded most abundantly to silence their shamelessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which sort of thing He did also with respect to the Sabbath, bringing forward David, the priests, the testimony that saith, “I will have mercy, and not sacrifice,” the cause of the Sabbath, for which it was ordained; “for the Sabbath,” saith He,” was for man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This then He doth in the present case also: where after the first He proceeds to a second refutation, plainer than the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For if I,” saith He, “by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your sons cast them out?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See here too His gentleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not, “my disciples,” nor, “the apostles,” but “your sons;” to the end that if indeed they were minded to return to the same noblenesswith them, they might derive hence a powerful spring that way; but if they were uncandid, and continued in the same course, they might not thenceforth be able to allege any plea, though ever so shameless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what He saith is like this, “By whom do the apostles cast them out?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in fact they were doing so already, because they had received authority from Him, and these men brought no charge against them; their quarrel not being with the acts, but with the person only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As then it was His will to show that their sayings arose only from their envy against Him, He brings forward the apostles; thus: If I so cast them out, much more those, who have received their authority from me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless, no such thing have ye said to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then bring ye these charges against me, the author of their doings, while acquitting them of the accusations?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This, however, will not free you from your punishment, rather it will condemn you the more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also He added, “They shall be your judges.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when persons from among you, and having been practised in these things, both believe me and obey, it is most clear that they will also condemn those who are against me both in deed and word.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But if I cast out devils by the Spirit of God, then the Kingdom of God is come unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What means “the Kingdom”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “My coming.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how again He conciliates and soothes them, and draws them to the knowledge of Himself, and signifies that they are warring with their own good, and contentious against their own salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For whereas ye ought to rejoice,” saith He, “and leap for joy, that One is come bestowing those great and unutterable blessings, hymned of old by the prophets, and that the time of your prosperity is at hand; ye do the contrary; so far from receiving the blessings, you do even speak ill of them, and frame accusations that have no real being.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now Matthew indeed saith, “If I by the Spirit of God cast out”; but Luke, “If I by the finger of God cast out the devils:”implying that to cast out devils is a work of the greatest power, and not of any ordinary grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He means indeed that from these things they should infer and say, If this be so, then the Son of God is come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This, however, He saith not, but in a reserved way, and so as not to be galling to them, He darkly intimates it by saying, “Then the kingdom of God is come unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou exceeding wisdom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By the very things which they were blaming, He showed His presence shining forth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, to conciliate them, He said not simply, “The Kingdom is come,” but, “unto you,”as though He had said, To you the good things are come; wherefore then feel displeased at your proper blessings? why war against your own salvation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is that time, which the prophets long ago foretold: this, the sign of that advent which was celebrated by them, even these things being wrought by divine power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the fact indeed, that they are wrought, yourselves know; but that they are wrought by divine power, the deeds themselves cry out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and it is impossible that Satan should be stronger now; rather he must of absolute necessity be weak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But it cannot be, that he who is weak should, as though he were strong, cast out the strong devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now thus speaking He signified the power of charity, and the weakness of separation and contentiousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He was Himself also continually charging His disciples, on every occasion, concerning charity, and teaching them that the devil, to subvert it, leaves nothing undone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having then uttered His second refutation, He adds also a third, thus saying:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “How can one enter into the strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man, and then spoil his goods?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that Satan cannot possibly cast out Satan is evident from what hath been said; but that neither in any other way is it possible to cast him out, except one first get the better of him, this too is acknowledged by all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is established hereby?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The former statement, with more abundant evidence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Why, I am so far,” saith He, “from using the devil as an ally, that I make war upon him, and bind him; and an infallible proof thereof is the plundering of his goods.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how the contrary is proved, of what they were attempting to establish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas they wished to show, that not by His own power doth He cast out devils, He shows that not only the devils, but even their very chief leader is held by Him bound with all authority; and that over him, before them, did He prevail by His own power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is evident from the things that are done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if he be the prince, and they subjects, how, except he were worsted, and made to bow down, could they have been spoiled?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And here His saying seems to me to be a prophecy likewise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not only, I suppose, are the evil spirits the goods of the devil, but also the men that are doing his works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore to declare that He doth not only cast out devils, but also will drive away all error from the world, and will put down his sorceries, and make all his arts useless, He said these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said not, He will take away, but “He will spoil,” to express what is done with authority.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He calls him “strong,” not because he is so by nature, God forbid, but declaring his former tyranny, which arose from our remissness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “He that is not with me is against me, and he that gathereth not with me scattereth abroad.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Behold also a fourth refutation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what is my desire? saith He.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To bring men to God, to teach virtue, to proclaim the kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What, that of the devil, and the evil spirits?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The contrary to these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then should he that gathers not with me, nor is at all with me, be likely to co-operate with me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why do I say co-operate?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, on the contrary, his desire is rather to scatter abroad my goods.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He then who is so far from cooperating that he even scatters abroad, how should he have exhibited such unanimity with me, as with me to cast out the devils?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now it is a natural surmise that He said this not of the devil only, but Himself also of Himself, as being for His part against the devil, and scattering abroad his goods.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how, one may say, is he that is not with me against me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By this very fact, of his not gathering.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if this be true, much more he that is against him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if he that doth not co-operate is an enemy, much more he that wages war.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But all these things He saith, to indicate His enmity against the devil, how great and unspeakable it is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me, if thou must go to war with any one, he that is not willing to fight on thy side, by this very fact is he not against thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if elsewhere He saith, “He that is not against you is for you,”it is not contrary to this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For here He signified one actually against them, but there He points to one who in part is on their side: “For they cast out devils,” it is said “in Thy name.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But to me He seems here to be hinting also at the Jews, setting them on the devil’s side.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they too were against Him, and were scattering what He gathered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As to the fact that He was hinting at them also, He declared it by speaking thus,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Therefore I say unto you, that all manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus having defended Himself, and refuted their objection, and proved the vanity of their shameless dealings, He proceeds to alarm them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this too is no small part of advice and correction, not only to plead and persuade, but to threaten also; which He doth in many passages, when making laws and giving counsel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And though the saying seem to have much obscurity, yet if we attend, its solution will prove easy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: First then it were well to listen to the very words: “All manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men; but the blasphemy of the Holy Ghost shall not be forgiven unto them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whosoever speaketh a word against the Son of Man, it shall be forgiven him; but whosoever speaketh against the Holy Ghost, it shall not be forgiven him, neither in this world, neither in the world to come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What now is it that He affirms?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Many things have ye spoken against me; that I am a deceiver, an adversary of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things I forgive you on your repentance, and exact no penalty of you; but blasphemy against the Spirit shall not be forgiven, no, not to those who repent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how can this be right?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even this was forgiven upon repentance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Many at least of those who said these words believed afterward, and all was forgiven them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is it then that He saith?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That this sin is above all things unpardonable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because Himself indeed they knew not, who He might be, but of the Spirit they received ample experience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the prophets also by the Spirit said whatever they said; and indeed all in the Old Testament had a very high notion of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What He saith, then, is this: Be it so: ye are offended at me, because of the flesh with which I am encompassed: can ye say of the Spirit also, We know it not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And therefore is your blasphemy unpardonable, and both here and hereafter shall ye suffer punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For many indeed have been punished here only (as he who had committed fornication,as they who partook unworthily of the mysteries,amongst the Corinthians); but ye, both here and hereafter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now as to your blasphemies against me, before the cross, I forgive them: and the daring crime too of the cross itself; neither shall ye be condemned for your unbelief alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: (For neither had they, that believed before the cross, perfect faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And on many occasions He even charges them to make Him known to no man before the Passion; and on the cross He said that this sin was forgiven them.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as to your words touching the Spirit, they will have no excuse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in proof that He is speaking of what was said of Him before the crucifixion, He added, “Whosoever shall speak a word against the Son of Man, it shall be forgiven him; but whosoever shall speak against the Holy Ghost,” there is no more forgiveness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because this is known to you; and the truths are notorious which you harden yourselves against.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though ye say that ye know not me; yet of this surely ye are not ignorant, that to cast out devils, and to do cures, is a work of the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is not then I only whom ye are insulting, but the Holy Ghost also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore your punishment can be averted by no prayers, neither here nor there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so of men, some are punished both here, and there, some here only, some there only, others neither here nor there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here and there, as these very men (for both here did they pay a penalty, when they suffered those incurable ills at the taking of their city, and there shall they undergo a very grievous one), as the inhabitants of Sodom; as many others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There only, as the rich man who endured the flames,and had not at his command so much as a drop of water.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here, as he that had committed fornication amongst the Corinthians.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Neither here nor there, as the apostles, as the prophets, as the blessed Job; for their sufferings were not surely in the way of punishment, but as contests and wrestlings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us labor, therefore, to be of the same part with these: or if not with these, at least with them that wash away their sins here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For fearful indeed is that other judgment, and inexorable the vengeance, and incurable the punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou desire not to be punished even here, pass judgment on thyself, exact thine own penalty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Listen to Paul, when he saith, “If we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thou do this, proceeding in order thou shalt even arrive at a crown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But how are we to exact our own penalty? one may ask.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Lament, groan bitterly, humble, afflict thyself, call to remembrance thy sins in their particulars.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This thing is no small torture to a man’s soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If any man hath been in a state of contrition, he knows that the soul is punished by this more than anything.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If any hath been living in remembrance of sins, he knows the anguish thence arising.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore doth God appoint righteousness as a reward for such repentance, saying, “Be thou first to tell thy sins, that thou mayest be justified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is not, it is not indeed, a small step towards amendment, to lay together all our sins, and to be continually revolving and reckoning them up with their particulars.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he that is doing this will be so heart-broken, as not to think himself worthy so much as to live; and he that thinks thus, will be tenderer than any wax.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me not of acts of fornication only, nor of adulteries, nor of these things that are manifest, and acknowledged amongst all men: but lay together also thy secret crafts, and thy false accusations, and thine evil speakings, and thy vain gloryings, and thine envy, and all such things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither will these bring a trifling punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the reviler too shall fall into hell; and the drunkard hath no part in the kingdom; and he that lovest not his neighbor so offends God, as to find no help even in his own martyrdom; and he that neglects his own hath denied the faith, and he who overlooks the poor is sent into the fire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Account not then these things to be little, but put all together, and write them as in a book.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou write them down, God blots them out; even as on the other hand, if thou omit writing them, God both inscribes them, and exacts their penalty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It were then far better for them to be written by us, and blotted out above, than on the contrary, when we have forgotten them, for God to bring them before our eyes in that day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore that this may not be so, let us reckon up all with strictness, and we shall find ourselves answerable for much.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who is clear from covetousness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, tell me not of the quantity, but since even in a small amount we shall pay the same penalty, consider this and repent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who is rid of all insolence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet this casts into hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who hath not secretly spoken evil of his neighbor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet this deprives one of the Kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who hath not been self-willed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet this man is more unclean than all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who hath not looked with unchaste eyes?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet this is a complete adulterer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who hath not been “angry with his brother without a cause”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet such an one is “in danger of the council.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who hath not sworn?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet this thing is of the evil one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who hath not forsworn himself? but this man is something more than of the evil one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who hath not served mammon? but this man is fallen away from the genuine service of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I have also other things greater than these to mention: but even these are enough, and able, if a man be not made of stone, nor utterly past feeling, to bring him to compunction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if each one of them casts into hell, what will they not bring to pass when all are met together?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then can one be saved? it may be asked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By application of the countervailing remedies: alms, prayers, compunction, repentance, humility, a contrite heart, contempt of possessions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For God hath marked out for us innumerable ways of salvation, if we be willing to attend.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us then attend, and let us every way cleanse out our wounds, showing mercy, remitting our anger against them that have displeased us, giving thanks for all things to God, fasting according to our power, praying sincerely, “making unto ourselves friends of the mammon of unrighteousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so shall we be able to obtain pardon for our offenses, and to win the promised good things; whereof may we all be counted worthy, by the grace and love toward man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might forever and ever.
